name: Hiram Bullock
abstract: Hiram Law Bullock (September 11, 1955 – July 25, 2008) was a Japanese-born Afro American guitarist known mainly for playing in jazz funk and jazz fusion, but he also worked as a session musician in a variety of genres.

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Lundy
about_quotes.evidence: "Where's Hiram? Hiram Bullock? 'Oh he moved to New York.' I'm thinking what is this New York thing and what is everybody doing going to this New York place? ... I didn't know there was this, this was the hub of the jazz world. I had no idea."
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Lundy interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Josiah Gluck
about_quotes.evidence: the house band consisted of Omar Harkim, Marcus Miller, Phillipe Saisse, Hiram Bullock ... and David, and yeah I did part of the first season
about_quotes.doc_title: Josiah Gluck interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 13, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Levin
about_quotes.evidence: People — Hiram Bullock came into the band at one point... I came into the band... we had a great time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Levin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001
